title: Strategies for international marketing
summary: 9609 A Level — export, franchising, joint ventures, FDI, and glocalisation of the marketing mix.
learningObjectives: Describe modes of international market entry.
Compare control, cost, and risk of each mode.
Recommend international strategy for case firms.

simpleExplanation.title: Selling beyond borders
simpleExplanation.summary: International marketing can start with exporting (low risk, limited control), then franchising, joint ventures, or direct investment — usually adapting product and promotion to local culture.
steps: Export: sell abroad with minimal local presence.
Franchising/licensing: brand and system to local operator.
Joint venture: shared ownership with local partner.
Adapt product and promotion to local tastes (glocalisation).

flashcards.back: Lowest cost entry; limited market knowledge and control.
Franchisee runs local outlets to brand standards — McDonald's model.
Shared investment/risk with local firm — useful where law requires local partner.
Own subsidiary abroad — high control and cost.
Same marketing mix globally — economies of scale.
Modify product/promotion for local culture — higher relevance.
Legal, political, cultural differences (6.1) drive adaptation.
Overseas revenue value fluctuates — finance consideration (5.2).

sections.content: **International marketing** (8.2.3) completes **Topic 8.2**. Compare **entry modes** on **control vs cost vs risk**; cite **local adaptation** examples from the case.

sections.question: Premium cosmetics firm considers entering India. Options: online export only, franchise with local retailer, or 50/50 joint venture with Indian beauty group. Recommend an approach.
sections.solution:
**Export only:** **Low risk** but **no local shop experience** for premium brand; **import duties** raise price.

**Franchise:** **Faster rollout**, local **retail expertise**; **quality control** risk if franchisee deviates.

**Joint venture:** **Shared market knowledge**, navigate **regulations**; **profit split**, **conflict** possible (7.1.4 trust).

**Adaptation:** **Shades and formulations** for Indian skin tones; **modest promotion** vs Western imagery — **glocalisation**.

**Recommendation:** **JV or master franchise** for physical presence; **adapt product line**; keep **global brand identity**.
